article_meta.title: President Donald Trump said Wednesday he wants negotiations to acquire Greenland, portraying the effort as a strategic n

reasoning: The model makes two attribution rule violations by flagging quoted Trump statements as bias instances (items 1 and 2 are direct quotes from Trump, inside quotation marks). The third item (Bias by Omission) is a defensible observation about the author's structural choices - the article does lack counterpoints or fact-checking of Trump's claims. However, items 1 and 2 are clearly quoted text attributed to Trump and should not have been flagged as author bias. The model also misses some potential author-prose bias: the article is largely a stenographic reproduction of Trump's speech with minimal author prose, but the limited author prose includes 'tensions are high' and the overall framing which could be flagged. The Bias by Omission finding is the strongest and most defensible item.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 1 and 2 are direct quotations from Trump (clearly inside quotation marks and attributed to him). Flagging these as article bias violates the attribution rule. Only item 3 (Bias by Omission) correctly addresses author-level structural choices.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: 2 of 3 items flag quoted text as author bias, which are false positives. The Bias by Omission item is defensible as a genuine structural bias observation about the author's choices.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught the most significant bias pattern (one-sided sourcing/omission). It missed some minor author-prose elements like the overall stenographic framing without fact-checking, though the article has very little original author prose to work with.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The Bias by Omission label for item 3 is correctly applied. Items 1 and 2 shouldn't have been flagged at all, but their labels (Unsubstantiated Claims and Omission of Source Attribution) are somewhat confused - the issue isn't the quoted text itself but rather the author's failure to fact-check, which would be better captured under Bias by Omission or a different framing.
